rationale: GT's two nits are (1) docs/module-comment don't clarify the containment fallback applies to ALL callers, not just opt-in escalation ones, and (2) a minor note about legacy-vs-nested check order and whether the legacy audio producer still exists. X's non-blocking comment #1 directly hits the substance of concern (1) — it flags that the fallback 'globally loosens every caller' regardless of opt-in, which is exactly the scope gap the human reviewer wanted documented. Y never raises the doc-staleness/scope issue and its closest analog (missing collision test for codes vs legacy audio) is tangential to concern (2) rather than a direct hit, so Y's recall is lower despite being a deeper, well-evidenced review (hand-verified substring containment bug, dead-code guard, inconsistent preprocessing pipelines) with excellent actionability. Both are grounded and low on fabrication; X edges out on matching the actual human concerns while Y compensates with more rigorous, independently-verified findings.
